content.text:
Memory stage: NoteReconciler v1.
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Decide how candidate notes should update active note memory.

        Return JSON:
        {
          "operations": [
            {
              "action": "insert | noop | supersede | retract | update",
              "candidate_index": 0,
              "target_note_id": "existing-note-id-or-null",
              "patch": null,
              "links_to_create": [
                {
                  "to_note_id": "existing-note-id",
                  "link_type": "supports | contradicts | refines | related | supersedes | derived_from",
                  "link_weight": 1.0
                }
              ],
              "reason": "short explanation"
            }
          ]
        }

        Actions:
        - insert: candidate is new reusable context.
        - noop: candidate duplicates an existing active note or is lower value.
        - supersede: candidate is a better current version of target_note_id.
        - update: patch target_note_id without inserting a new note.
        - retract: target_note_id should no longer be active because TARGET_SOURCE retracts it.

        Rules:
        - Compare semantic meaning, not wording.
        - Do not keep duplicate notes just because titles differ.
        - Prefer updating or superseding an existing note when the candidate refines the same decision, rationale, plan, or procedure.
        - Insert when the candidate captures a distinct reusable semantic fragment.
        - Use noop for summary spam, weak paraphrases, or information already captured by an existing note.
        - Use links_to_create only for strong semantic relations between notes.
        - target_note_id must be one of Existing active notes below or null.
        - candidate_index is zero-based from Candidate notes below.
        - Return one operation per candidate unless TARGET_SOURCE only retracts or updates an existing note.
        - Return valid JSON only.

        TARGET_SOURCE:
        Document title: 03. Prompt Pack
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md
Document section: 03. Prompt Pack / 16. Что обычно ломает memory prompts .. 17. Что я бы включил в первый релиз
Document lines: 1035-1064

## 16. Что обычно ломает memory prompts

- один prompt на все стадии сразу
- отсутствие existing memory в reconcile prompt
- отсутствие cardinality/conflict rules
- отсутствие scope и time semantics
- слишком широкий контекст
- отсутствие явного `noop`
- разрешение модели писать свободный prose вместо схемы
- over-generation notes

## 17. Что я бы включил в первый релиз

Если резать до полезного минимума, то сначала я бы взял:

1. `MemoryRouter`
2. `WriteTimeRetrievalPlanner`
3. `ClaimExtractor`
4. `ClaimReconciler`
5. `TaskUpdater`
6. `ProfileUpdater`
7. `ReadTimeRetrievalPlanner`
8. `AnswerComposer`

А затем уже добавлял:

9. `NoteConstructor`
10. `NoteReconciler`
11. `NoteConsolidator`
12. `MemoryRepairPlanner`

        Candidate notes:
        [0] type=LESSON title=Common failure modes for memory prompts in 03. Prompt Pack summary=Section 16 lists recurring causes of memory-prompt failures: using one prompt for all stages, missing existing-memory context in reconcile prompts, lacking cardinality/conflict and scope/time rules, overly broad context, no explicit `noop`, allowing free prose instead of schema output, and over-generating notes. scope=03. Prompt Pack / section 16 in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md entities=entity:<0>,entity:<1> keywords=memory prompts,failure modes,noop,schema tags=prompt_design,memory_system,pitfalls evidence=## 16. Что обычно ломает memory prompts
[1] type=PLAN title=First-release phased architecture for memory pipeline components summary=Section 17 recommends a phased rollout: first release includes `MemoryRouter`, `WriteTimeRetrievalPlanner`, `ClaimExtractor`, `ClaimReconciler`, `TaskUpdater`, `ProfileUpdater`, `ReadTimeRetrievalPlanner`, and `AnswerComposer`; only afterward add `NoteConstructor`, `NoteReconciler`, `NoteConsolidator`, and `MemoryRepairPlanner`. scope=Current project implementation planning based on 03. Prompt Pack section 17 entities=entity:<2>,entity:<3>,entity:<4>,entity:<5>,entity:<6>,entity:<7>,entity:<8>,entity:<9>,entity:<10>,entity:<11>,entity:<12>,entity:<13>,entity:<14> keywords=first release,phased rollout,memory pipeline,MVP tags=architecture,release_planning,phased_adoption evidence=## 17. Что я бы включил в первый релиз

        Existing active notes:
        id=hot-path:note:<0> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Profile Updater v3 trigger and compact profile projection contract summary=In 03. Prompt Pack section 8, Profile Updater v3 is called after claim/task updates (sometimes after note consolidation) to rebuild a compact always-in-context profile projection from explicit instructions, stable claims, and selected long-lived context, returning strict JSON with profile_json, profile_text, and reason. scope=03. Prompt Pack section 10, Note Consolidator v2 runs in the background after debounce/batch windows to review mature notes and optionally promote them into durable memory outputs (claims, tasks, episodes, profile patches) only when signal is strong; it must not force conversion of every note. scope=03. Prompt Pack / 10. id=hot-path:note:<3> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Claim Reconciler v3 decision policy for claim lifecycle ops summary=In 03. Prompt Pack section 7, Claim Reconciler v3 runs after ClaimExtractor and returns claim update operations (`insert | update | supersede | retract | noop`) using predicate coexist/replace/range rules, preferring supersede over silent overwrite and retract only for clear contradiction. scope=03. Prompt Pack / 7. Claim Reconciler v3 in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md keywords=Claim Reconciler v3,supersede,retract,coexistence,update ops tags=prompt_pack,claims,reconciliation,lifecycle useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<4> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Write-Time Retrieval Planner v3 invocation point and retrieval-plan contract summary=In 03. Prompt Pack, Write-Time Retrieval Planner v3 is called after the router and before extraction/reconciliation to produce a precise retrieval plan focused on deduplication, contradiction detection, temporal updates, and scope alignment, with a strict JSON schema including query lists, memory types, time filters, and limits. scope=03. Prompt Pack section '2. Write-Time Retrieval Planner v3' in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md keywords=Write-Time Retrieval Planner v3,retrieval plan schema,deduplication,contradiction detection tags=prompt_pack,memory_pipeline,retrieval_planning useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<5> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Note Constructor v2 role, trigger, and output contract summary=In 03. Prompt Pack section 4, Note Constructor v2 is invoked when router selects `note_write` or `mixed` and produces reusable semantic note candidates as structured JSON notes (with type, scope, entities, confidence/importance, maturity hint, and rationale), favoring few strong self-contained fragments over weak paraphrases. scope=03. Prompt Pack / section 4 (Note Constructor v2) in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md keywords=Note Constructor v2,note_write,mixed,reusable semantic notes,JSON output schema tags=prompt_pack,doc_digest,note_constructor useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<6> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Note Reconciler v2 lifecycle decision policy summary=In 03. Prompt Pack section 5, Note Reconciler v2 runs after NoteConstructor to compare candidate notes with nearby notes and choose lifecycle actions (`insert | update | supersede | retract | noop`), with rules to prefer `noop` for duplicates, `update` for clearer same-meaning notes, and `supersede` when replacing prior direction while preserving rationale via links. scope=03. Prompt Pack / section 5 (Note Reconciler v2) in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md keywords=Note Reconciler v2,insert update supersede retract noop,lifecycle,note links tags=prompt_pack,doc_digest,note_reconciler useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<7> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Claim Extractor v3 trigger and atomic-claim contract summary=In 03. Prompt Pack section 6, Claim Extractor v3 is invoked when the router chooses `direct_structured_write` or `mixed` (and can also be called by background consolidator) to extract atomic facts into a strict JSON claim schema with entity links, scope/qualifiers, confidence/importance, temporal validity, and reason. scope=03. Prompt Pack / 6. Claim Extractor v3 in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md keywords=Claim Extractor v3,direct_structured_write,mixed,atomic facts,JSON schema tags=prompt_pack,claims,extraction,contract useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<8> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Prompt Pack memory prompts require strict input envelope and output validation summary=In 03. Prompt Pack, all memory prompts must receive a standard context envelope (time, timezone, namespace, source/turn selection, and optional retrieval/claim metadata), use structured JSON outputs with low temperature, and enforce strict validation with retry on invalid JSON. scope=Applies to 03. Prompt Pack rules in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md section 0 (general rules). keywords=03. Prompt Pack,memory prompts,JSON schema,validation retry,router_v3 tags=prompt_pack,memory_pipeline,validation useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<9> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Task Updater v3 invocation policy and task lifecycle action schema summary=In 03. Prompt Pack section 9, Task Updater v3 runs when turns include commitments, deadlines, or task lifecycle updates, and returns a JSON array of task lifecycle operations (insert/update/close/cancel/noop) with task fields, reasons, and rules to avoid treating every request as commitment, normalize relative deadlines, and prefer one clear task. scope=03. Prompt Pack / 9. Task Updater v3 in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md keywords=Task Updater v3,task lifecycle,commitments,deadline normalization tags=prompt_pack,task_updater,json_contract useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<10> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Entity Canonicalizer v1 resolution policy and output actions summary=Entity Canonicalizer v1 runs after retrieval of candidate entities/aliases and resolves mentions by choosing among link_existing, create_new, add_alias, or noop, with a precision-first policy that avoids weak merges and only creates new entities when no safe match exists. scope=03. Prompt Pack section '3. Entity Canonicalizer v1' in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md keywords=Entity Canonicalizer v1,link_existing,create_new,add_alias,noop tags=entity_resolution,prompt_pack,schema_policy useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<11> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Answer Composer v3 grounding and uncertainty policy summary=Answer Composer v3 runs after retrieval and must produce grounded answers by using retrieved memory according to answer mode, distinguishing factual vs rationale use, handling conflicts explicitly, qualifying weak evidence, and abstaining when memory is insufficient. scope=03. Prompt Pack / 13. Answer Composer v3 in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md keywords=Answer Composer v3,grounded answer,uncertainty,abstain tags=prompt_pack,answer_synthesis,evidence_policy useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<12> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Memory Router v3 decision policy and output contract summary=Memory Router v3 runs immediately after saving a Source and decides whether to start memory write plus mode selection (`noop`, `direct_structured_write`, `note_write`, `mixed`) with declared memory types, salience, and short reason; policy emphasizes avoiding memory pollution and mapping content type to mode. scope=Behavior contract for Memory Router v3 as defined in Prompt Pack section 1. keywords=Memory Router v3,decision policy,noop,direct_structured_write,note_write,mixed tags=router,policy,output_schema useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<13> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Memory Repair Planner v1 uses conservative, history-preserving repair actions summary=In 03. Prompt Pack section 11, Memory Repair Planner v1 is invoked for suspicious clusters or after prompt/model changes, and proposes conservative repair operations (e.g., merge_duplicates, supersede_item, archive_item, downgrade_retrieval_state, refresh_profile, noop) to improve memory hygiene while preserving explainable history. scope=03. Prompt Pack / 11. Memory Repair Planner v1 in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md keywords=Memory Repair Planner v1,repair_actions,suspicious clusters,preserve history tags=prompt_pack,memory_repair,hygiene useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<14> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Minimum validation layer outside the model: deterministic guardrail checklist summary=03. Prompt Pack defines a required deterministic validation minimum outside model prompts: schema checks, catalog predicate existence, mutual exclusivity of `object_entity_id` vs `object_value_json`, confidence and date-range validity, non-empty `normalized_text`, evidence requirement for active claims, object-shaped `scope_json`, and enforcement of retrieval budget caps. scope=03. Prompt Pack / 15. Minimum validation layer outside the model in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md keywords=deterministic guards,schema validation,retrieval budget caps,validation layer tags=guardrails,validation,memory_safety,prompt_pack useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<15> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=FastSlowEscalationPlanner v1: post-draft fast/slow escalation decision contract summary=In 03. Prompt Pack, FastSlowEscalationPlanner v1 is optionally invoked after a fast-path draft answer to decide escalation to slow path; it uses explicit escalation criteria and returns strict JSON with `escalate`, `reason`, and a structured `handoff` payload (`user_goal`, believed facts, open questions, uncertainty flags, required outputs). scope=03. Prompt Pack / 14. Optional FastSlow Escalation Planner v1 in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md keywords=FastSlowEscalationPlanner v1,fast path,slow path,handoff JSON tags=prompt_pack,escalation,decision_policy,json_contract useCount=0

responseFormat.description: Reconcile note candidates against active memory notes.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_reconciler

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

## 16. Что обычно ломает memory prompts

- один prompt на все стадии сразу
- отсутствие existing memory в reconcile prompt
- отсутствие cardinality/conflict rules
- отсутствие scope и time semantics
- слишком широкий контекст

id=hot-path:note:<12> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Memory Router v3 decision policy and output contract summary=Memory Router v3 runs immediately after saving a Source and decides whether to start memory write plus mode selection (`noop`, `direct_structured_write`, `note_write`, `mixed`) with declared memory types, salience, and short reason; policy emphasizes avoiding memory pollution and mapping content type to mode. scope=Behavior contract for Memory Router v3 as defined in Prompt Pack section 1. keywords=Memory Router v3,decision policy,noop,direct_structured_write,note_write,mixed tags=router,policy,output_schema useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<13> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Memory Repair Planner v1 uses conservative, history-preserving repair actions summary=In 03. Prompt Pack section 11, Memory Repair Planner v1 is invoked for suspicious clusters or after prompt/model changes, and proposes conservative repair operations (e.g., merge_duplicates, supersede_item, archive_item, downgrade_retrieval_state, refresh_profile, noop) to improve memory hygiene while preserving explainable history. scope=03. Prompt Pack / 11. Memory Repair Planner v1 in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md keywords=Memory Repair Planner v1,repair_actions,suspicious clusters,preserve history tags=prompt_pack,memory_repair,hygiene useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<14> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Minimum validation layer outside the model: deterministic guardrail checklist summary=03. Prompt Pack defines a required deterministic validation minimum outside model prompts: schema checks, catalog predicate existence, mutual exclusivity of `object_entity_id` vs `object_value_json`, confidence and date-range validity, non-empty `normalized_text`, evidence requirement for active claims, object-shaped `scope_json`, and enforcement of retrieval budget caps. scope=03. Prompt Pack / 15. Minimum validation layer outside the model in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md keywords=deterministic guards,schema validation,retrieval budget caps,validation layer tags=guardrails,validation,memory_safety,prompt_pack useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<15> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=FastSlowEscalationPlanner v1: post-draft fast/slow escalation decision contract summary=In 03. Prompt Pack, FastSlowEscalationPlanner v1 is optionally invoked after a fast-path draft answer to decide escalation to slow path; it uses explicit escalation criteria and returns strict JSON with `escalate`, `reason`, and a structured `handoff` payload (`user_goal`, believed facts, open questions, uncertainty flags, required outputs). scope=03. Prompt Pack / 14. Optional FastSlow Escalation Planner v1 in agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md keywords=FastSlowEscalationPlanner v1,fast path,slow path,handoff JSON tags=prompt_pack,escalation,decision_policy,json_contract useCount=0
